title: Final Claim Architecture

claims.level1_empirical: On the quality-controlled N=45 SPARC sample with published distances, galaxy-to-galaxy variation in inferred a0 is better described by a structured five-axis power law (M5) than by a strict universal constant, with 46.6% of inter-galaxy variance explained under leave-one-out cross-validation.
claims.level2_predictive: M5 outperforms the universal-constant model and all simpler alternatives in prediction, produces quantitative falsifiable forecasts (24/26 passed), and succeeds in controlled matched-pair tests between individual galaxies (22/25 correct sign, median |error| = 0.165 dex).
claims.level3_physical: The best framework-neutral physical interpretation is that a0 behaves as an emergent state variable of the galaxy, governed by gas content, host-system gravitational depth, and baryon/kinematic organization — not as a strict universal constant of nature.
claims.level4_frontier: Beyond M5, no additional clean scalar axis is extractable from the current data. The remaining 0.189 dex residual is a featureless Gaussian frontier with no subpopulation bias, no heteroscedasticity, and no bimodality.

explicitClaims: a0 is not a strict universal constant on N=45
The variation is not random scatter — it is structured
Best current predictive law is M5 (5-axis, LOO=51%)
Best compressed state law is M3 (3-axis, LOO=44%)
a0 reads as a state-dependent effective acceleration scale
MHI and Mhost carry independent information
The kinematic axis has a real 2D dynamical origin
The residual frontier is structureless Gaussian
explicitNonClaims: This is the final cosmic law for all galaxies
Dark matter has been definitively confirmed
No intrinsic residual exists beyond measurement noise
The blind external test confirmed generalization
This replaces the RAR or BTFR

wordings.conservative: On the current N=45 high-quality sample, inferred galaxy-to-galaxy a0 variation is better described by a structured five-axis law than by a strict universal constant.
wordings.strong: Inferred a0 variation on the N=45 quality sample is structured, predictive, and pairwise-testable; it is not well described as either a strict universal constant or unstructured galaxy-to-galaxy scatter.
wordings.physics: The data favor interpreting a0 as an emergent, state-dependent galaxy parameter rather than a strictly universal acceleration scale.

evidenceTable.evidence: M0 loses to M5/M3 on LOO, AIC, BIC, k-fold, bootstrap (Phase 69)
Structured model repeatedly outperforms null on all validation metrics
Phase 69 death match: M5 defeats all competitors, LOO=46.6%
Phase 68-69: 3 axes retain 95% of M5, LOO=44.1%
Phase 72: 22/25 controlled pairs pass (88%), median |err|=0.165 dex
Phase 70: fgas collapse loses -36.5pp; exponents -1/4 vs -1/6
Phase 74: MeanRun correlates |r|=0.83 with 2D THINGS dynamics
Phase 69: F=1.50, 14.5% sign flips, hurts LOO
Phase 75: 0/7 strat biases, BP=4.63, JB=1.22, Gaussian
